title: The MD/chem/bio family (openmm, mdanalysis, rdkit, sella, vina, pdbfixer) as used by AtomisticSkills
scanner_note: SCAN stage only: catalog, not map code. Target family: the classical-MD / molecular-informatics / structural-biology tooling the drug-* and chem-* skills drive. Honest scoping is the whole game: encode what carries map-relevant physics (MM-MD trajectory observables, TS/barrier energies, BDE), catalog-only with a stated reason for informatics/bio tooling (SMILES/descriptors/conformers/fingerprints/logP, docking scores, PDB structure prep). Every claim is anchored to a committed AtomisticSkills file:line. openmm/mdanalysis/rdkit/sella/vina are NOT importable in the miniconda base env; MDAnalysis 2.10.0 base-unit table was read from the pip-downloaded wheel, and every skill-usage / unit claim is anchored to the vendored skill scripts.

graph_snapshot.head: 2c9e1ac
graph_snapshot.note: graph.json ships 77 nodes and an empty edges array at scan HEAD. Nodes already present that this family produces or borders: Trajectory, Temperature, Pressure, TotalEnergy, Forces, Stress, Structure, MeanSquaredDisplacement, Diffusivity, ActivationEnergy, ReactionEnergy, AdsorptionEnergy, FormationEnergy, Frequency, ElectricalConductivity[carrier=ionic]. Re-read graph.json at encode time; the structure-gen and amset tails have been landing.

sources.provenance: openmm, MDAnalysis, rdkit, sella, vina, pdbfixer, openbabel, biopython are NOT importable in /Users/juicy/miniconda3/bin/python (base env). MDAnalysis 2.10.0 was pip-downloaded as a wheel to /tmp/mdsrc and its units.py base-unit table read directly (Angstrom, ps, kJ/mol, kJ/(mol*Angstrom)). openbabel and biopython: NO import anywhere under .agents/skills/ (grep clean); pdbfixer/biopython structure prep is served by pdbfixer + openmm.app.Modeller, not biopython. All engine/quantity/unit claims below anchor to committed skill scripts, not to captured output files.

rdkit: many more as a SMILES<->3D/identity layer
biopython: NONE (no import anywhere under .agents/skills/; structure I/O is pdbfixer + openmm.app + MDAnalysis)

sources.engine_note: The chem-* MD (chem-solution-md, chem-neb-barrier, chem-ts-optimization, chem-bond-dissociation) run on MLIPs through ASE (get_potential_energy/get_forces, eV/Angstrom), the matcalc-scan domain: their physics rides the existing MLIP Potential rail. The drug-* MD (drug-protein-ligand-md, drug-mmpbsa-gbsa) run on CLASSICAL force fields through OpenMM (kJ/mol, kelvin, atm): a NEW engine class with a NEW checkpoint analog (the force-field set), distinct from the MLIP PES.

molar_energy_factor_set.note: This family adds a THIRD and FOURTH molar-energy convention to the map's ledger. OpenMM AND MDAnalysis both work in kJ/mol; Vina and MM-GBSA and BDE report kcal/mol; the MLIP/ASE routes stay in eV. Exact factors below (CODATA-2018 e = 1.602176634e-19, N_A = 6.02214076e23).
molar_energy_factor_set.openmm_native: kJ/mol for energy (unit.kilojoule_per_mole), kelvin for temperature, atmosphere for pressure, femtosecond for timestep, nanometer for length, kJ/mol/nm^2 for restraint force constants. run_md.py:44 PE reported in kJ/mol; :47 T in K; :234 pressure in atm; :77,343 restraint_k in kJ/mol/nm^2.
molar_energy_factor_set.mdanalysis_native: Angstrom (length), ps (time), kJ/mol (energy), kJ/(mol*Angstrom) (force), Angstrom/ps (speed). From MDAnalysis 2.10.0 units.py MDANALYSIS_BASE_UNITS. RMSD/RMSF/COM outputs are Angstrom; MSD is Angstrom^2; time axis is ps. NOTE the length/time base (Angstrom, ps) differs from OpenMM (nm, fs) even though both use kJ/mol energy: a units bridge at the OpenMM->MDAnalysis handoff.
molar_energy_factor_set.vina_native: kcal/mol (empirical scoring-function score, reported as affinity_kcal_mol; run_docking.py:153,161).
molar_energy_factor_set.bde_native: eV (MLIP) converted to kcal/mol and kJ/mol in-script (calculate_bde.py:486-488, EV_TO_KCAL_MOL=23.0605 truncated).

exact_factors.cross_check_hartree: 1 Hartree = 2625.499639479 kJ/mol = 627.5094740631 kcal/mol = 27.211386245988 eV (the ORCA-scan factor, for the shared molecular-energy ledger)
exact_factors.basis_reminder: kJ/mol and kcal/mol here are per-mole-of-MOLECULES (N=-1 for a whole molecule, dimension ENERGY_PER_MOLE), distinct from the CALPHAD per-mole-of-ATOMS and phonon per-mole-of-cells nodes. An OpenMM PE in kJ/mol for a solvated box is per-SIMULATION-CELL (extensive) already expressed molar-of-that-cell: reading it as a molar energy needs the same basis label the ORCA scan flagged.

molar_energy_factor_set.truncation_trap: chem-bond-dissociation uses EV_TO_KCAL_MOL=23.0605 (calculate_bde.py:58) and its own EV_TO_KJ_MOL literal, both truncated from the exact 23.060547830619 / 96.48533212331. Sub-1e-4 relative error; record the exact factors on the encode side.

entries.engine: openmm (a FOURTH Trajectory-producing engine after gpumd, lammps, ase.md)
entries.native_units: positions nm, DCD frames; time fs timestep; written via app.DCDReporter
entries.physics: Newtonian dynamics on a classical MM force field (LangevinMiddleIntegrator, run_md.py:177,202,237,277). Four-phase workflow: minimize -> restrained NVT -> restrained-then-released NPT -> production NPT. A genuine Trajectory in the map's sense, but on a fitted force field rather than an MLIP or DFT PES.
entries.proposal: Same Trajectory node. The engine label gains 'openmm' and the provenance MUST record the force-field set (see openmm-forcefield-provenance) as the checkpoint analog, exactly as an MLIP trajectory records its checkpoint.
entries.trap: positions are nm (OpenMM) but MDAnalysis reads them into Angstrom; DCD stores its own unit convention. The Trajectory is thermostatted (Langevin) and barostatted (MonteCarloBarostat, run_md.py:234), so instantaneous frames are canonical-ensemble samples, not a microcanonical NVE trace.

entries.physics: Kinetic temperature from equipartition, 2*KE/(N_dof*k_B). Same Temperature node the LAMMPS/gpumd thermal scans already ground.
entries.proposal: Same Temperature node; the target T is a thermostat setpoint (control parameter), the reported T is the observable. No new node.
entries.trap: run_md.py:42 divides by 3N*R without removing constrained/COM degrees of freedom, a small-system bias; the StateDataReporter temperature (:213) uses OpenMM's own dof-corrected value. Two slightly different T definitions in the same script.

entries.quantity: MM potential energy (and total energy) of the simulation cell
entries.grounds_node: none exact; sibling of TotalEnergy on a classical MM basis
entries.driven_by: run_md.py:44 (PE via getPotentialEnergy().value_in_unit(kilojoule_per_mole)), :190-191 minimized PE, StateDataReporter potentialEnergy=True/totalEnergy=True (:216-219, :308-311)
entries.per_what: PER SIMULATION CELL (solvated protein-ligand box), extensive, kJ/mol. This is a fourth ENERGY basis in the same shape as the map TotalEnergy 'one scalar per cell' but on a CLASSICAL FORCE-FIELD zero, not a DFT/MLIP zero.
entries.physics_vs_periodic: SAME shape (extensive energy per cell) as TotalEnergy, DIFFERENT physics origin: a sum of bonded + nonbonded (PME electrostatics, LJ) force-field terms, NOT a quantum PES. The absolute zero is force-field-defined and MUST NOT be subtracted against MLIP/DFT totals. A thermostatted PE fluctuates; its ensemble mean is the reportable quantity.
entries.proposal: Either a labeled TotalEnergy(basis=per_cell, engine=openmm, potential_class=classical_forcefield) OR a distinct MM energy node. The load-bearing job of the label is to forbid cross-substrate subtraction (MM vs MLIP vs DFT energy zeros). This mirrors the ORCA-scan per-molecule/per-cell TotalEnergy basis-label recommendation. The skills report PE/total-energy only as equilibration diagnostics (density/T convergence), not as a standalone deliverable, so the node is capability-level for now.
entries.trap: kJ/mol (OpenMM) vs eV (map); force-field energy zero not comparable across codes; the reported number is thermostatted/barostatted, an ensemble sample not a ground-state minimum.

entries.physics: NPT barostat targets 1 atm; StateDataReporter emits system density in g/cm^3. The map has a Pressure node (mechanical/stress-trace family). Density (g/cm^3) has NO map node; it is a derived mass/volume convergence diagnostic.
entries.proposal: Pressure setpoint -> existing Pressure node (control-parameter label). Density -> minor Density candidate (see chem-solution-md-density entry), currently a convergence check, not a deliverable.
entries.trap: the barostat pressure is a control setpoint in atm; the map's Pressure node is a mechanical observable (stress trace) typically in GPa/bar. Do not conflate the setpoint with a measured virial pressure.

entries.quantity: Force-field set (protein FF + ligand FF + water model) as the MM checkpoint analog
entries.grounds_node: none (a provenance/gauge label on every openmm-produced node)
entries.driven_by: drug-complex-system-builder/scripts/build_complex.py:97 protein_ff='amber/ff14SB', :98 water_model='tip3p', :50-63 openmmforcefields SMIRNOFF/GAFF generator (openff-2.2.0 default, gaff-2.11 option), :110-117 water_ff_map {tip3p, tip3pfb, tip4pew, opc, spce}, :133 app.ForceField(protein_ff, water_ff), :186 nonbondedMethod=PME
entries.physics: The (protein_ff, ligand_ff, water_model, nonbondedMethod) tuple is to an OpenMM MM-MD run exactly what a checkpoint is to an MLIP run and a (functional, basis_set, pseudopotential) is to a DFT run: the method provenance that every derived energy/force/trajectory rides on. openmmforcefields is the provenance registry (SystemGenerator that assigns ligand parameters via SMIRNOFF openff or GAFF).
entries.proposal: Representation-only: this is a scheme/gauge on the openmm-produced Trajectory/energy/force nodes, NOT a node. Record the full FF string (protein_ff, ligand_ff, water_model, nonbondedMethod, box_shape, ion concentration) in provenance. Water model is provenance, not a separate quantity: tip3p vs opc changes every downstream number.
entries.trap: the ligand FF is auto-assigned per molecule by openff/GAFF; two runs with 'the same protocol' can differ if the openff version moves. The water model (tip3p default) is the single biggest provenance knob and is NOT a physical property of the system.

entries.quantity: Ligand RMSD, pocket RMSF, ligand-protein COM drift
entries.driven_by: drug-trajectory-analysis/scripts/analyze_trajectory.py:79-97 RMSD via MDAnalysis.analysis.rms.RMSD, :163-176 RMSF via align.AverageStructure+AlignTraj+rms.RMSF, :101-131 COM drift
entries.per_what: RMSD/RMSF are single-molecule structural fluctuation metrics of a BIOMOLECULE relative to a reference frame. They are geometric descriptors of binding-mode stability, not physical observables with a thermodynamic dimension the map tracks.
entries.physics_vs_map: These ARE well-defined geometric quantities (Angstrom deviations), but the map is a materials-physics quantity graph: RMSD/RMSF/COM drift are protein-ligand binding-stability descriptors with no counterpart node and no path into any map operator. MSD (a cousin) IS mapped (diffusion probe); RMSD/RMSF are NOT MSD (they measure deviation-from-reference, not diffusive displacement).
entries.proposal: CATALOG-ONLY. Reason: bio-structural descriptors of a single macromolecule's conformational stability, dimensionally LENGTH but semantically outside the materials-physics map. No node, no operator.
entries.trap: do not confuse RMSF/RMSD (Angstrom, deviation from a reference structure) with MeanSquaredDisplacement (Angstrom^2, diffusive spread) which the map DOES carry. Different physics, different dimension.

entries.quantity: Protein-ligand hydrogen-bond occupancy, residue contact occupancy
entries.driven_by: drug-trajectory-analysis/scripts/analyze_trajectory.py:192-247 HydrogenBondAnalysis (d_a_cutoff 3.5 A, angle 120 deg), :250-295 contact occupancy via capped_distance (5 A cutoff)
entries.per_what: per residue-pair / per donor-acceptor pair, over a trajectory. Fraction of frames a geometric H-bond or contact criterion is met.
entries.physics_vs_map: Geometric interaction fingerprints for binding-mode analysis. Dimensionless occupancies, defined by distance/angle cutoffs. Pure structural-biology analysis with no materials-physics node.
entries.proposal: CATALOG-ONLY. Reason: bio-descriptor (geometric-criterion occupancy), no physical dimension the map tracks, no operator path.
entries.trap: occupancy depends entirely on the cutoff choices (3.5 A / 120 deg); it is a definition, not a measured quantity.

entries.driven_by: chem-solution-md/scripts/analyze_solution_md.py:215-284 compute_msd (MSD(t)=<|r(t)-r(0)|^2>, PBC-unwrapped, per element), SKILL.md:11,137 msd_elements
entries.per_what: atom-and-time-averaged squared displacement of a chosen element in a solvation box, exactly the map's MeanSquaredDisplacement 'diffusion probe' definition (registry.py:133).
entries.physics: Einstein MSD. Same node the LAMMPS/gpumd thermal-transport scans ground. Runs on an MLIP via ASE MD (chem-* engine class), NOT OpenMM. This is the RETURN of the old LAMMPS MSD candidate flagged in the task, now confirmed as an already-mapped node.
entries.proposal: Same MeanSquaredDisplacement node. NOTE: this skill computes the MSD CURVE only; it does NOT fit a Diffusivity slope (no D=MSD/6t or polyfit anywhere in analyze_solution_md.py). So it grounds MeanSquaredDisplacement but does NOT itself produce the map's Diffusivity node (that fit is agent-level or a downstream skill).
entries.trap: MSD here is Angstrom^2 (MDAnalysis-style length base) not nm^2; the map's Diffusivity node is NOT produced by this skill despite MSD being present, the Einstein slope is not fit in-script.

entries.quantity: Radial distribution function g(r) and coordination number
entries.grounds_node: none (g(r) is a curve; coordination number is a derived scalar)
entries.native_unit: g(r) dimensionless vs r (Angstrom); coordination number dimensionless (integer-ish count)
entries.driven_by: chem-solution-md/scripts/analyze_solution_md.py:33 compute_rdf (element-pair g(r), rmax 8 A, PBC), :127-176 compute_coordination_number (integral of 4*pi*r^2*rho*g(r) to first minimum), SKILL.md:121,132-133 rdf_pairs
entries.per_what: g(r) is a pair-correlation FUNCTION over a distance axis (a curve, per element pair); coordination number is its running integral to the first minimum (a scalar per pair).
entries.physics_vs_map: g(r) is a genuine structural correlation function of the liquid, real physics, but a CURVE not a scalar node (like PhononDOS or CumulativeKappa, which the map does carry as curve-valued nodes). Coordination number is a derived scalar. The map has NO RDF or coordination-number node today. This is the RDF the old LAMMPS candidate raised, resurfacing: it is representation-level structural output, not a currently-mapped quantity.
entries.proposal: REPRESENTATION-ONLY for now (candidate curve-valued node if the map decides to ingest liquid-structure descriptors). Reason: g(r) is a structure-factor-family curve with no operator today; coordination number is its integral. Neither feeds an existing map node. If ingested later, model g(r) like PhononDOS (curve node) and coordination number as its derived scalar.
entries.trap: g(r) is home-rolled here (NOT MDAnalysis.analysis.rdf.InterRDF), so its normalization/binning is skill-owned; coordination number depends on the first-minimum cutoff choice.

entries.driven_by: chem-solution-md/scripts/analyze_solution_md.py:177-213 compute_density_vs_time (total mass / cell volume, Angstrom->cm), SKILL.md:140,142 density_convergence.png; also openmm StateDataReporter density (run_md.py:255)
entries.per_what: bulk mass density of the simulation cell, g/cm^3, as a function of time (equilibration check).
entries.physics_vs_map: Density is a real bulk property (mass/volume) but the map has no Density node; here it is used ONLY as an NPT-equilibration convergence diagnostic (verify density stabilizes before production, SKILL.md:174). Not a deliverable quantity.
entries.proposal: REPRESENTATION-ONLY. Reason: equilibration diagnostic, not a produced observable; a Density node would be a minor candidate if the map ingests bulk-liquid properties. The map already carries CellVolume; density is trivially mass/CellVolume.
entries.trap: reported as a time series for convergence, not a single equilibrium value.

entries.engine: sella (order-1 saddle optimizer) on an MLIP via ASE, + ase.vibrations for validation
entries.driven_by: chem-ts-optimization/scripts/optimize_ts_sella.py:76 from sella import Sella, :96-102 Sella(atoms, order=1), :111 optimizer.run(fmax=0.02), :128-142 ase Vibrations -> signed cm^-1 -> is_first_order_saddle (exactly 1 imaginary mode); chem-irc-verification/scripts/verify_irc_sella.py (forward/reverse IRC connectivity)
entries.per_what: produces a RELAXED SADDLE-POINT Structure (order-1) plus the vibrational signature that certifies it (n_imag==1). It does NOT itself emit a barrier energy; it reports max_force (eV/A), all_frequencies_cm1, is_first_order_saddle.
entries.physics: Sella is the molecular (non-periodic) analog of matcalc NEBCalc / ase.mep: a TS search. It grounds Structure (the optimized TS) and Forces, and its imaginary-mode count is a molecular-Frequency-family label (the mode index kind the ORCA scan proposed). The BARRIER ENERGY is a downstream difference of MLIP total energies at reactant/product/TS (agent-level, same backend/model/head per SKILL.md:93).
entries.proposal: Structure + Forces already-mapped (TS geometry via MLIP). The saddle-order (n_imaginary) is a molecular-Frequency-family label (see the ORCA scan's molecular-Frequency mode-index proposal, do not duplicate). The barrier delta is the reaction-barrier node candidate below, which sella FEEDS (via TS energy) but does not itself compute.
entries.trap: sella emits geometry + frequencies, NOT a barrier scalar; the barrier is reactant/product/TS energy differences the agent assembles. Frequencies are signed cm^-1 (imaginary printed negative), the ORCA-scan molecular-Frequency mode-index convention.

entries.quantity: Reaction / migration barrier energy (the deferred NEB barrier + sella TS + ORCA static-TS convergence)
entries.grounds_node: none today (ReactionBarrier candidate); bordered by ActivationEnergy (Arrhenius-slope) and TotalEnergy(saddle)
entries.driven_by: chem-neb-barrier/scripts/calculate_barrier.py:10 from ase.mep import NEBTools, NEB, :117 NEBTools(images).get_barrier()[0], :143 results['barrier_eV']=barrier (CI-NEB, idpp interpolation, 7 images default, FIRE/BFGS, MLIP calculator). Fed ALSO by sella TS energy (sella-transition-state-geometry) and, on the molecular-QM side, by ORCA static-TS (orca-transition-state-energy, the ORCA scan's entry).
entries.per_what: the peak-minus-reactant energy along a minimum-energy path (NEB) or peak-minus-reactant from a static saddle (sella/ORCA). A single ENERGY scalar per reaction.
entries.physics_vs_map: This is the barrier-energy node the matcalc verdict EXPLICITLY DEFERRED TO THIS TASK (chem-neb-barrier drives raw ase.mep, not matcalc NEBCalc). It is DISTINCT from the map's ActivationEnergy, which registry.py:138 defines as the Arrhenius activation energy from the T-dependence of diffusivity (a slope, not a PES barrier). Three producer routes converge on ONE barrier-energy candidate: (a) CI-NEB minimum-energy-path barrier (chem-neb-barrier, eV, MLIP); (b) static saddle-point barrier from sella TS + reactant/product energies (chem-ts-optimization, eV, MLIP); (c) static saddle from ORCA molecular DFT (orca-transition-state-energy, Hartree->eV). Keep them ONE node kept apart by a construction label {neb_mep, static_ts_mlip, static_ts_dft}.
entries.proposal: NEW-NODE-CANDIDATE: a ReactionBarrier node (ENERGY, eV), fed by the NEB route (chem-neb-barrier, THE deferred one), the sella static-TS route, and (bordering, do NOT duplicate) the ORCA static-TS route the ORCA scan already catalogs. Kept apart from Arrhenius ActivationEnergy by construction label. This is the ONE barrier-energy node the task asked to gather across sella + ase.mep + ORCA.
entries.overlap_note: The ORCA scan's orca-transition-state-energy entry is the molecular-DFT producer of the SAME barrier family. This entry is the MLIP/ASE producer (NEB + sella). They are one node, two engine routes; recorded here as an overlap, not a duplicate.
entries.trap: NEB barrier is eV (MLIP); ORCA static-TS barrier is Hartree->eV (all-electron zero); they must not be numerically compared across the MLIP/DFT energy-zero split. Arrhenius ActivationEnergy (existing node) is a DIFFERENT quantity (a diffusivity-slope, not a PES barrier) despite the tempting name collision.

entries.grounds_node: none exact; sibling of ReactionEnergy (a total-energy difference)
entries.dimension: ENERGY (eV native; also kcal/mol, kJ/mol in-script)
entries.native_unit: eV (MLIP), converted to kcal/mol (x23.0605) and kJ/mol
entries.driven_by: chem-bond-dissociation/scripts/calculate_bde.py:8 BDE_homo = E(A.) + E(B.) - E(A-B), :486-490 bde_eV/bde_kJ_mol/bde_kcal_mol; rdkit does the fragmentation (:233 enumerate_bonds, :276 fragment_molecule), the MLIP supplies energies (:220 get_potential_energy)
entries.per_what: the energy to homolytically (or heterolytically) cleave one bond: a difference of relaxed total energies (fragments minus intact). A single ENERGY scalar per bond.
entries.physics_vs_map: SAME physics-shape as the map's ReactionEnergy (a stoichiometric total-energy difference), but ReactionEnergy is defined as a solid-state balanced-reaction energy from per-atom formation energies (registry.py:137). A molecular BDE is a molecule-fragment energy difference on the MLIP zero. rdkit's role is purely informatics (bond enumeration and fragmentation); the PHYSICS (the energy) is the MLIP total-energy difference.
entries.proposal: NEW-NODE-CANDIDATE: a molecular BondDissociationEnergy (ENERGY, eV), a labeled sibling of ReactionEnergy (cleavage_type in {homolytic, heterolytic}, basis=per_molecule). Do NOT overload the solid-state ReactionEnergy node; the basis differs (molecular fragments vs balanced solid-state reaction). rdkit is catalog-only here; only the energy difference is map-worthy.
entries.trap: homolytic BDE needs open-shell fragment energies (radicals: charge/spin set at calculate_bde.py:162); heterolytic needs charged fragments. The MLIP must handle spin/charge (wrapper_supports_mace_charge check, :156). Truncated EV_TO_KCAL_MOL=23.0605.

entries.quantity: MM-GBSA / MM-PBSA endpoint binding free energy estimate
entries.driven_by: drug-mmpbsa-gbsa/scripts/compute_mmgbsa.py:8 dG = E_complex - E_receptor - E_ligand, :123 build_implicit_system (OpenMM GBn2 implicit solvent), :314-350 per-frame dG_kcal, dG_mean/std/sem
entries.per_what: single-trajectory endpoint free-energy estimate: the mean over trajectory frames of (complex - receptor - ligand) implicit-solvent MM energies. Per protein-ligand complex.
entries.physics_vs_map: A PHYSICS-based estimator (MM force field + GBn2/PB continuum solvation), unlike the purely empirical Vina score, BUT the skill itself states the goal is 'relative ranking rather than absolute binding affinity' (compute_mmgbsa.py:11): it omits entropy and configurational sampling corrections. It is a semi-empirical endpoint free energy, more physical than a docking score, less rigorous than alchemical FEP.
entries.proposal: NEW-NODE-CANDIDATE with a strong caveat: a BindingFreeEnergy node LABELED (method=mmgbsa|mmpbsa, endpoint, single_trajectory, implicit_solvent=GBn2) that forbids treating it as an absolute affinity. It IS force-field physics (unlike Vina), so it is a candidate rather than pure catalog-only, but its intended use is RANKING. Decide whether the map ingests protein-ligand binding thermodynamics at all (opens the drug-* domain).
entries.trap: kcal/mol; single-trajectory (no separate complex/receptor/ligand sampling) so cancellation of errors is assumed; the internal_dielectric knob (compute_mmgbsa.py:444) is a major ranking lever; NOT an absolute affinity.

entries.dimension: reported as kcal/mol but is an EMPIRICAL SCORE, not a physical free energy
entries.driven_by: drug-docking-vina/scripts/run_docking.py:143 v.energies(), :153 affinity_kcal_mol=total, :161 best_affinity_kcal_mol; scoring functions vina/vinardo/ad4 (:212-216)
entries.per_what: the value of a FITTED empirical scoring function (weighted sum of pairwise atom-type terms + torsion penalty) at the best docked pose, per ligand.
entries.physics_vs_map: The task's crux question answered with facts: Vina's 'affinity' is the output of an EMPIRICAL SCORING FUNCTION regressed against experimental binding data (Vina/Vinardo/AD4 term weights), reported in kcal/mol units but NOT a computed thermodynamic free energy. It is a rank-ordering score, not physics. AD4 columns even include a '-intra' bookkeeping term (run_docking.py:82-84). This is fitted-model output, exactly like logP.
entries.proposal: CATALOG-ONLY. Reason: empirical fitted scoring function, not a physical observable. The kcal/mol label is nominal (calibrated to correlate with affinity), not a computed free energy. Contrast mmgbsa-binding-free-energy, which IS force-field physics (candidate). Vina score is NOT a map quantity.
entries.trap: the kcal/mol units are misleading: a Vina score is NOT a free energy. Ligand efficiency (LE/BEI/SEI, drug-docking-analysis/scripts/analyze_docking.py:50-70) is derived from this score + rdkit descriptors and is equally catalog-only.

entries.grounds_node: none (the CONFORMER GEOMETRY grounds Structure only after MLIP relaxation, which is a separate already-mapped step)
entries.per_what: 3D coordinate sets generated from a SMILES/2D graph by the ETKDG distance-geometry heuristic, optionally relaxed with the MMFF/UFF empirical force field.
entries.physics_vs_map: ETKDG is a knowledge-based distance-geometry SAMPLER (torsion preferences from crystal data), not a physics engine; MMFF/UFF are cheap empirical force fields used only to clean up embeddings. In these skills the rdkit conformer is a STARTING GEOMETRY that is then relaxed with an MLIP (conformer_search.py:154 get_potential_energy in eV). The map-worthy output is the MLIP-relaxed Structure + its eV energy (already-mapped), NOT the rdkit conformer or its MMFF energy.
entries.proposal: CATALOG-ONLY for the rdkit/MMFF layer. Reason: molecular-identity-to-3D representation generation (informatics + empirical FF), not a physical quantity. The downstream MLIP relaxation IS already-mapped (Structure + TotalEnergy) but that is the MLIP's contribution, not rdkit's.
entries.trap: the MMFF/UFF energy rdkit reports (kcal/mol) is an empirical-FF number and is discarded once the MLIP relaxes the geometry; do not mistake the ETKDG/MMFF conformer energy for a physical energy.

entries.driven_by: drug-molecular-fingerprints (Morgan fingerprints, Tanimoto/Dice similarity), chem-similarity-search, drug-db-* similarity queries
entries.per_what: a bit-vector encoding of substructure presence (Morgan/ECFP) and a set-overlap similarity (Tanimoto) between two such vectors.
entries.physics_vs_map: A fingerprint is a REPRESENTATION OF MOLECULAR IDENTITY (which substructures are present), and Tanimoto is a cheminformatics similarity metric. Neither is a physical quantity: they carry no dimension, no thermodynamics, no dynamics. This is the clearest catalog-only case.
entries.proposal: CATALOG-ONLY. Reason: representation of molecular identity / a similarity metric, dimensionless, no physics. Not a map quantity.
entries.trap: none physics-wise; a similarity is a definition, not a measurement.

entries.quantity: Molecular descriptors (MW, cLogP, TPSA, QED) and ADMET predictions
entries.driven_by: drug-docking-analysis/scripts/analyze_docking.py:58,70 Descriptors.TPSA / cLogP for ligand efficiency; drug-admet-prediction (ADMET model outputs); rdkit.Chem.Descriptors throughout
entries.per_what: atom-counting / group-contribution descriptors (MW, TPSA) and empirical-model predictions (cLogP, QED, ADMET endpoints) computed from the 2D molecular graph.
entries.physics_vs_map: The task singled out logP: cLogP (Crippen) is an EMPIRICAL GROUP-CONTRIBUTION MODEL output (a fitted regression over atom-type contributions), NOT a computed partition free energy. QED, TPSA, and ADMET endpoints are likewise fitted/counting descriptors. All are molecular-identity-derived empirical numbers, the logP-is-empirical-model-output case the task named.
entries.proposal: CATALOG-ONLY. Reason: empirical-model outputs / graph-counting descriptors of molecular identity, not physical observables. logP specifically is a fitted partition-coefficient model, not a solvation-free-energy computation. Not map quantities.
entries.trap: cLogP LOOKS like a thermodynamic partition coefficient but is a regression output; a computed solvation/partition free energy (which would be map-adjacent) is a DIFFERENT thing the skills do not compute here.

entries.quantity: Protein structure preparation (missing atoms/residues, protonation, nonstandard residues)
entries.grounds_node: none (produces a repaired Structure/topology as INPUT to MD, not an observable)
entries.driven_by: drug-protein-prep/scripts/prepare_protein.py:31 from pdbfixer import PDBFixer, :166 handle_missing_residues, :215 replace_nonstandard_residues, :242 apply_heterogen_policy, :269 openmm.app.Modeller for hydrogens
entries.per_what: a cleaned, complete, protonated protein topology + coordinates, ready for force-field assignment. A structure-PREPARATION step, not a measurement.
entries.physics_vs_map: PDBFixer repairs crystallographic PDBs (adds missing heavy atoms/loops, models hydrogens at a target pH, swaps nonstandard residues). The output is a Structure-family object, but it is an INPUT-preparation artifact for downstream MD, carrying no computed physical quantity. Analogous to structure generation, but for biomolecules and without any energy/property output.
entries.proposal: CATALOG-ONLY. Reason: structure-preparation tooling (input conditioning for MM-MD), no physical observable produced. The prepared topology feeds openmm-md-trajectory; only the downstream MD produces map quantities. biopython/openbabel would live here too but are NOT used by any skill.
entries.trap: protonation is pH-dependent (a modeling choice, not a measurement); nonstandard-residue swaps change the system identity. All provenance, no observable.

traps: kJ/mol vs kcal/mol vs eV vs Hartree, exact factors: 1 eV = 96.48533212331 kJ/mol = 23.060547830619 kcal/mol; 1 kcal/mol = 4.184 kJ/mol (exact thermochemical); 1 Hartree = 2625.499639479 kJ/mol = 627.5094740631 kcal/mol = 27.211386245988 eV. OpenMM AND MDAnalysis both use kJ/mol energy; Vina/MM-GBSA/BDE report kcal/mol; MLIP/ASE routes stay in eV.

OpenMM (nm, fs, kJ/mol) vs MDAnalysis (Angstrom, ps, kJ/mol): the energy base matches but the LENGTH and TIME bases differ across the handoff. RMSD/RMSF/MSD come out in Angstrom / Angstrom^2 with a ps time axis; the OpenMM trajectory was written in nm with fs timesteps.

OpenMM energy conventions: the potential energy is a classical force-field number on a force-field-defined zero (kJ/mol, can be large negative for a solvated box). It MUST NOT be subtracted against MLIP or DFT totals (four incompatible energy zeros now: MM force-field, MLIP, DFT-pseudopotential, DFT-all-electron/ORCA).

Vina 'affinity_kcal_mol' is an EMPIRICAL SCORE, not a free energy, despite the kcal/mol label. MM-GBSA dG IS force-field physics but is a single-trajectory endpoint estimate for RANKING, not an absolute affinity.

Water model as provenance: tip3p (default) vs tip3pfb/tip4pew/opc/spce (build_complex.py:110-117) changes every downstream number; it is a method knob, not a system property. Ligand FF (openff-2.2.0 vs gaff-2.11) and protein FF (amber/ff14SB) complete the MM checkpoint analog.

chem-solution-md computes MSD (Angstrom^2) and RDF g(r) and coordination number and density, but does NOT fit a Diffusivity slope: MeanSquaredDisplacement is grounded, Diffusivity is not produced by this skill despite being map-adjacent.
RMSD/RMSF (Angstrom, deviation from reference) are NOT MeanSquaredDisplacement (Angstrom^2, diffusive spread): different physics, different dimension, do not conflate.

sella emits a TS GEOMETRY + signed cm^-1 frequencies + saddle-order, NOT a barrier scalar; the barrier is a downstream reactant/product/TS energy difference. Frequencies signed (imaginary negative), the ORCA-scan molecular-Frequency mode-index convention.
cLogP looks thermodynamic but is an empirical Crippen group-contribution regression, not a computed partition free energy (the logP-is-empirical case).
open_questions: ReactionBarrier node: mint ONE barrier-energy node fed by the deferred NEB route (chem-neb-barrier, ase.mep, eV, THE matcalc-deferred one), the sella static-TS route (chem-ts-optimization), and the ORCA static-TS route (orca-transition-state-energy, the ORCA scan's entry, do NOT duplicate), kept apart from Arrhenius ActivationEnergy by construction label {neb_mep, static_ts_mlip, static_ts_dft}? (Recommended: yes, one node, three engine routes, cross-checked against the ORCA scan.)

MM engine class: OpenMM is a NEW engine (classical force field) distinct from MLIP and DFT. Its Trajectory is already-mapped, but its PE/total-energy sit on a fourth energy zero. Does the map ingest MM potential energy as a labeled TotalEnergy (basis=per_cell, engine=openmm, potential_class=classical_forcefield) or leave it capability-only (the skills use it only as an equilibration diagnostic)?

Force-field set as checkpoint analog: record (protein_ff, ligand_ff, water_model, nonbondedMethod) as the MM provenance on every openmm-produced node, mirroring the MLIP-checkpoint and DFT-method provenance. openmmforcefields is the registry. Confirm placement.

BondDissociationEnergy: mint a molecular ENERGY node (labeled sibling of solid-state ReactionEnergy, cleavage_type homolytic|heterolytic, basis=per_molecule) fed by chem-bond-dissociation via MLIP total-energy differences? rdkit is catalog-only (fragmentation); only the energy is map-worthy.

BindingFreeEnergy (MM-GBSA): ingest as a labeled candidate (method=mmgbsa, endpoint, single_trajectory, ranking-only) or defer the entire protein-ligand thermodynamics domain? It IS force-field physics (unlike Vina) but intended for ranking.

Liquid-structure descriptors: does the map ever ingest RDF g(r) (curve-valued node like PhononDOS), coordination number (derived scalar), and Density (mass/CellVolume)? Currently representation-only convergence diagnostics from chem-solution-md.

Diffusivity from solution MD: chem-solution-md grounds MeanSquaredDisplacement but does NOT fit the Einstein slope. Is there a downstream skill (or agent step) that produces the map's Diffusivity node from this MSD, and does that reintroduce the ionic-conductivity Nernst-Einstein route already in the graph?

Molecular-Frequency mode index: sella and the ORCA scan both produce discrete molecular normal modes (cm^-1, no q/branch). Confirm the single molecular-Frequency variant (index kind 'mode', imaginary count as label) the ORCA scan proposed absorbs the sella frequencies too (do not mint a second molecular-frequency node).

Catalog-only confirmation: rdkit (conformers/fingerprints/descriptors/logP), Vina docking score, ligand efficiency, RMSD/RMSF/hbonds/contacts, pdbfixer prep are all catalog-only with stated reasons (informatics representations of molecular identity / empirical fitted models / bio-structural descriptors / input conditioning). openbabel and biopython are unused by any skill. Confirm none of these enter map provenance.
Snapshot drift: scanned HEAD 2c9e1ac, 77 nodes, empty edges. Re-read graph.json at encode time; the structure-gen deep-review and any concurrent ORCA/encode work may have moved node counts and tags.
